scope.summary: Replace the remaining legacy blind-box category library with the validated 20-entry scene-plus-purpose model, authored in four pools and still consumed through the existing four runtime buckets.
in: Expand four-pool authoring data from the 3 pilot bundles to all 20 scene entries in Game content extraction/data/blind_boxes.py.
Define an explicit compatibility path from the new authoring model to the existing BLIND_BOXES runtime shape and comma-input flow.
Extend tests from pilot-only assertions to full-library coverage, including deprecated-pool absence and blocked-pattern scanning.
Sync required docs: agents.md, README.md, Game content extraction/CLAUDE.md, Game content extraction/README.md, .gitignore.
out: UI redesign or new input controls.
Web, database, service-side, or third-party UI dependencies.
Release packaging, APP_VERSION bump, or installer changes.
Expression, image fetcher, and batch renamer behavior changes outside doc sync.

current_state.catalog: BLIND_BOX_SCENE_ENTRIES already defines 20 scene-plus-purpose entries with scene hints and pilot flags.
current_state.authoring_model: BLIND_BOX_ITEM_POOL_BUNDLES currently contains only 3 four-pool pilot bundles: 桌面+学习, 海底+潜水, 公园+野餐.
current_state.runtime_model: BLIND_BOXES is still the old 20-box runtime library, with only ids 15, 16, and 17 overwritten by pilot-compatible generated entries.
current_state.compatibility: BLIND_BOX_COMPATIBILITY_MAPPING is pilot-only and BLIND_BOX_PILOT_BOX_IDS remaps pilot names to legacy runtime ids 15/16/17.
current_state.tests: Game content extraction/test_blind_box_content_model.py validates the 20-entry catalog but only asserts full four-pool coverage for the 3 pilot bundles.

implementation_steps: Replace the pilot-only bundle registry with a 20-entry four-pool registry keyed by the scene-plus-purpose names from BLIND_BOX_SCENE_ENTRIES, using the 3 validated pilots as quality templates.

Introduce or expand a stable mapping layer that connects each runtime box id to one new scene entry, then build BLIND_BOXES from the new four-pool source instead of mixing old static boxes with pilot overrides.

Preserve the current runtime bucket contract by continuing to emit large/medium/small/hanging, with large sourced from core_items + scene_expansion_items, medium sourced from support_items plus a bounded core fallback, small sourced from visible_small_items, and hanging kept as a compatibility bucket.

Decide and encode the history-safe mapping explicitly: either preserve existing legacy ids through a dedicated mapping table, or document the semantic drift and migration impact if ids are reassigned to the new 20-entry catalog.

Rewrite the remaining 17 scene libraries with the same content rules already used by the validated pilots: concrete visible objects only, no deprecated pools, visible_small_items must be grouped/blocky/supported, scene_expansion_items must be medium-or-larger and not anchor-dependent.

Expand regression tests to all 20 scenes, assert exact four-pool keys for every bundle, verify compatibility mapping coverage for every runtime box, and continue scanning all default pools for blocked patterns and risky terms.
Sync the required docs so they describe the full 20-entry four-pool library as current behavior rather than future direction, and keep .gitignore changes limited to real cache/build clutter only.
acceptance_criteria: Every scene entry in BLIND_BOX_SCENE_ENTRIES has a corresponding non-empty four-pool bundle.
No bundle contains conditional_items, anchor_required_items, or blocked_or_risky.
BLIND_BOXES still exposes the existing runtime schema for every reachable box id: name, large, medium, small, hanging.
Comma-input extraction remains usable without UI changes, and draw_history compatibility is either preserved by explicit mapping or covered by a documented migration decision.
Full-library tests pass after broadening coverage beyond the 3 pilots, including blocked-pattern scans over all default pools.
agents.md, README.md, Game content extraction/CLAUDE.md, Game content extraction/README.md, and .gitignore are synchronized with the implemented state.
risks: The biggest design risk is id/history semantics: the new 20-entry catalog currently uses ids 1-20, while the validated pilot rollout preserved runtime behavior by overriding legacy ids 15/16/17 only.

A full 20-category rewrite is content-heavy and easy to regress in quality; the remaining 17 categories need the same visible-object discipline as the pilots or blocked-pattern tests will be insufficient.
If BLIND_BOXES is regenerated wholesale, any hidden assumptions in UI defaults, saved history, or manual user habits around legacy names/ids may surface as regressions.
Doc sync is mandatory in this repo; forgetting agents.md, README.md, Game content extraction/CLAUDE.md, or .gitignore would leave the codebase internally inconsistent even if tests pass.
If .gitignore is expanded to cover tracked cache/build outputs, the implementation task must also clean tracked entries instead of only editing ignore rules.
recommendation: Implement IMPL-001 as a full-library data/model rewrite in one shared pass over blind_boxes.py, then update tests and docs immediately after. Do not treat this as another pilot-only patch.
